creator: noisetube@gmail.com
text:
That sounds fine. My patch was more of a workaround/PoC.

And yes, like I said, I'm using FreeBSD 12.x, and the port/package for that release is still based on GZDoom 4.1.1. I also downloaded and built the source for 4.6 myself (that was the current version back in July, when I first started looking at this) and I was able to get it to run. So in theory maybe upgrading the 12.x version of the port to the latest GZDoom is also an option.

Also, regarding the other issue you run into with Mesa, I've noticed that with both 4.1.1 and my locally build 4.6.0 version, I sometimes see this:

D_CheckNetGame: Checking network game status.
player 1 of 1 (1 nodes)
Using video driver x11
X connection to localhost:0 broken (explicit kill or server shutdown).

It's intermittent though: if I run it enough times it eventually works. It's not related to this issue though. (Hope you can eventually track it down.)
